channel.title: SCP: The Boy In The Mirror
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "SCP: The Boy In The Mirror" - A heartwarming one, for a change.

item.title: Re: SCP: The Boy In The Mirror
item.content:encoded:
Apologies for the lateness on this, life has that effect sometimes.

+ Line-by-Line
– hide block

Right off the bat, I'm a little on the ambivalent on the whole containment procedures thing. I get that they're outdated, but the jarring break from how it's normally presented rubs me a little bit.

The note on the request for usage of SCP-113 also seems a little out of place. As far as I can see, it doesn't really have any real bearing on the main narrative of the article. Were there a more clear link, I'd wholeheartedly support the crosslink, but it just doesn't feel super relevant here, other than it vaguely suggesting that Hayes pushes the envelope when it comes to rules.

its voice becoming almost hysterical

Doesn't feel super clinical here. Something like "its voice edging on hysteria" might work a little better.

– hide block
+ General/Other
– hide block

Overall, I'd say this is a pretty solid piece. A little on the short side for the character development, but I'd still say that it's effective. Your dialogue is well-done, and feels damn solid.

Leaving the actual dispersion to a footnote rubs me a little bit—it could make for a great sending-off for the characters that've been established, but it'd be in character for Hayes to not record it.

– hide block

All in all, I'd say this is damn close to being ready—I look forward to seeing where you take this!

item.title: SCP: The Boy In The Mirror
item.content:encoded:
Hey, new draft that's semi-character driven. It's moderately short, I think? I have trouble gauging lengths for articles. It's at the top.

Things I'm looking for:

  • Any oddities in the dialogue.
  • Any general complaints about the formatting.
  • Some similar, character-driven articles and tales that I can link in the infobox (preferably not from Series 1).

I'd be very appreciative if someone could give it a critique.

Edit: Title changed to A Choice In Death, at the suggestion of FloppyPhoenix.
